mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical edge with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all historical easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without overheating, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a post-recession recovery phase reflects rapidly expanding corporate output capacity and earnings momentum, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a historical hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across all market cycles, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 65% of instances following CPI prints above 0.4%, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding real GDP confirms broad-based economic strength and durable consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in over 55% of historical cases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its six-month trend indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening corporate earnings outlook, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the moving average crossover fails to generate a statistically significant edge with a p-value above 0.05.
